pages.body: This high-energy pop-rock anthem drives forward at 143 BPM, weaving distorted guitar swells and drill-style hi-hats over deep 808 bass pulses to craft an urgent soundscape of toxic love. It chronicles a relationship doomed from the start, where domestic chaos and emotional neglect mask themselves as passion. Through dynamic duet vocals alternating between intimate female leads and gritty male replies, the song traces a journey from fatalistic resignation in the intro to cathartic release in the final chorus. The lyrics expose red flags on walls and secrets hidden behind phones, capturing the painful realization that love was merely a burden disguised as romance.
pages.styleSentence: A high-energy (143 BPM) pop-rock anthem driven by a driving F-major key, featuring distorted guitar swells and modern drill-style hi-hats layered over deep 808 bass pulses to create an urgent yet emotionally raw soundscape.

pages.text:
[Intro - Female Spoken, Close Mic]
Fucked [Intro]

[spoken male vocals]
Fuckup ed up from the from the beginnbeginning
ing...
Always was...
Always was
Always will be...

Always will be[Male Spoken, Low]
We should’ve seen it.

[Instrumental Break]

[distorted guitar swell, drill hats, 808 pulse]

[Verse 1 - Female Lead]
Fucked up from the jump, we were smiling through the sirens
Red flags on the walls, but we called it perfect timi[Male Spoken]
Always ng
I kept looking for the green, like a sign I could believe in
But the only green was money when the bills came screaming
You left socks on the floor like a war I couldn’t win
Unfinished water by the sink, same fight again
Laundry in a mountain, folding turned to blame
Overtime kept calling and we answered it by name

[Male Reply]
I was tired, you were distant
We were loud, never listening

[Female Lead]
You got secret with your phone, I got colder with my tone
Two people in a house still feeling alone

[Pre-Chorus - Female]
Eyes bloodshot
Not from the rot
But from reading every silence like a warning shot

[Male]
Stay in your lane

[Female]
But the lane was a graveyard
Every marker had our name
Every name had a scar

w[Chorus - Female Lead + Male Echo]
Maybe I as...

should stop
[Female Whisper]
AlMaybe I should drop
Let the mountain keep the weight I never asked to carry up
Maybe I should fall
Echo down the hall
Let the silence be the answer when I can’t stand up at all
Stop, drop
Watch it all dissolve
Stop, drop
Nothing left to solve

[Instrumental Break]

[guitar riff, 808 slide, snare build]

[Verse 2 - Female Lead]
We fought about the dishes, fought about the rent
Fought about the words we never really meant
You said I was dramatic, I said you never cared
Truth is we were both just swinging at the air

[Male Reply]
You checked out first

[Female Reply]
You locked up worse

[Male]
You wanted proof

[Female]
You hid the hurt

[Female Lead]
Had the green light gleaming, but it never meant go
Just another little lie in a pretty red glow
I should’ve seen the signs when love felt like a debt
When every kiss came loaded with a thing we won’t forget

[Pre-Chorus - Female + Male]
Eyes bloodshot
Not from the rot
Not from the drinks
Not from the smoke
But from the mirror showing both of us the joke

[Male]
We called it passion

[Female]
It was damage in disguise

[Both]
We kept feeding the fire
Then acted surprised

[Chorus - Female Lead + Male Echo]
Maybe I should stop
Maybe I should drop
Let the mountain keep the weight I never asked to carry up
Maybe I should fall
Echo down the hall
Let the silence be the answer when I can’t stand up at all
Stop, drop
Watch it all dissolve
wStop, drop
Nothing left to solve

[Bridge - Female, Vulnerable]
Hate to admit it
But God, I’m terrified
Tarred by my own hands
Feathered by my pride

[Male, Soft]
I loved you wrong

[Female]
I stayed too long

[Both]
We were doomed before the song

[Solo Full Band Instrumental Break]

[explosive guitar solo, crash cymbals, 808 undercurrent]

[Final Pre-Chorus - Female, Rising]
Eyes bloodshot
Not from the rot
Not from the mountain
Not from the cold
But from all those little warnings
That we watched and still ignored

[pause break]

[Final Chorus - Female Lead, Full Band]
Maybe I should stop
Maybe I should drop
Let the mountain keep the weight I never asked to carry up
aMaybe yI s should fall
will be...

[Ending Instrumental Break]

[clean guitar fading into distorted low 808]Echo down the hall
Let the silence be the answer when I can’t stand up at all
But the bloodshot fades
And the red flags wave
And the truth comes late
But it comes anyway

[Post-Chorus - Female + Male]
Maybe I should stop
Maybe I should drop
Maybe I already have

[Outro - Female Spoken]
Fucked up from the beginning...

[Male Spoken]
Always was...

[Female Whisper]
Always will be...

[Ending Instrumental Break]

[clean guitar fading into distorted low 808]

pages.vocalStyle: Dynamic duet with spoken-word intros and verses alternating between intimate close-mic female leads and gritty male replies, culminating in a soaring but weary chorus.

members.name: LEAD VOX
members.bio: She sings with close-mic intimacy and fierce clarity.

members.name: BACKING VOX
members.bio: Her backing harmonies lift the weary chorus higher still.

members.name: DRUMS
members.bio: Keeps the frantic four-on-the-floor shaking violently.

members.name: PERCUSSION
members.bio: Adds sharp drill-style hi-hats to drive urgency forward.

members.name: BASS
members.bio: Pulsing deep eighteighths anchor this anxious emotional ground.

members.name: SYNTH
members.bio: Weaves shimmering pads that soften the gritty reality around.

members.name: TEXTURES
members.bio: Textural layers create a murky, chaotic domestic atmosphere here.

members.name: GUITAR
members.bio: Distorted swells swell up like rising anger and pain inside.

members.name: KEYS
members.bio: Piano lines cut through chaos with melancholic precision.
